events.name: Homesteaders of America Conference (10th Anniversary)

dates.notes: Friday & Saturday weekend pass covers both days (approx 7:00 AM Fri to 4:00 PM Sat). Hands-on all-day workshops held Thursday, Oct 8 at the fairgrounds — NOT included in regular ticket price, advance reservation required, to be announced closer to spring. Free Homestead Revival service Friday at 6:30 PM (open to anyone, no ticket required).

events.description: 10th anniversary edition of the Homesteaders of America Conference, billed as the largest homestead conference in the nation. Includes a large vendor hall, hands-on demonstrations, expert-led talks on topics from beekeeping to greenhouse growing, paid Kidsteader classes (general kids 11 & under free with ticket), and a free Friday-evening Homestead Revival service. Optional all-day hands-on workshops on Thursday, Oct 8. Rain or shine; no refunds; tickets are non-transferable.
events.ticket_price: Single-Person Pass $50, Two-Person Pass $99; Children 11 & under free but require a ticket (listed $0–15) for head count. General admission only — Kidsteader classes & Thursday workshops cost extra.
highlights: vendor hall (largest homestead conference in the nation)

social_posts.facebook:
📍 Homesteaders of America Conference — 10th Anniversary
🗓️ October 9–10, 2026 (Friday & Saturday)
📌 Warren County Fairgrounds, Front Royal, VA
🎟️ Single Pass $50 | Two-Person Pass $99 | Kids 11 & under free (ticket still required for head count)

Ten years in, this has grown into the largest homestead gathering in the nation — and the folks in the movement keep showing up because it delivers the real thing.

Walk the biggest vendor hall you'll find anywhere in homesteading. Watch hands-on demos on the skills that actually matter — beekeeping, greenhouse growing, and more. Bring the little ones to the Kidsteader classes (general kids admission is free with a ticket). And on Friday at 6:30 PM, the free Homestead Revival service is open to anyone — no ticket needed.

Want to go deeper? The all-day hands-on workshops on Thursday, Oct 8 are worth the extra cost, but they require advance reservation and details drop closer to spring.

A heads up the homestead community already knows: these tickets sell out fast. Rain or shine, no refunds, non-transferable — so lock in your weekend before they're gone.

Grab your pass here 👉 https://homesteadersofamerica.com/product/conference-weekend-pass/

#Farm2Table #HomesteadingCommunity #HomesteadExpo #SelfSufficiency

events.name: Modern Homesteading Conference (4th Annual)

dates.notes: Two-day conference Friday June 26 & Saturday June 27, 2026. Confirmed by official site. There IS a Sunday morning add-on: an all-attendee 'Cowboy Church' worship service led by Joel Salatin on Sunday morning (included with all tickets). Note: one ticket-page URL slug reads 'June 26-28' and a third-party calendar listed June 27-28, but the official site, FAQ, and Facebook all consistently state June 26 & 27, with Cowboy Church Sunday morning. The 'possible Sunday extension' in the prior record is effectively the Sunday Cowboy Church. Full session schedule to be released in early 2026.

location.address: 4056 N Government Way, Coeur d'Alene, ID 83815

events.description: The 4th annual Modern Homesteading Conference is a two-day, hands-on event at the Kootenai County Fairgrounds in Coeur d'Alene, ID, focused on practical self-sufficiency skills, live demonstrations (including live butchering both days, animal care, gardening, and food preservation), a vendor hall/homestead marketplace, food trucks, and community-building. Co-founded by Melissa K. Norris and Katie Millhorn. Features 40+ (third-party listings cite ~70) experts. New for 2026: a dedicated Youth Track for ages 12-18, plus a Friday Community Meet & Greet and Sunday morning Cowboy Church led by Joel Salatin.
events.ticket_price: General Admission $149/person (children 2 and under free); Children's GA (ages 3-17) $49; General Admission Family Pass $399 (2 adults + 5-10 children); VIP Admission $399. Early-bird discounts observed (e.g. $49 off adult GA; a $129 early-access promo). All tickets are non-refundable and include access to all speakers, vendors, demos, food trucks, Joel Salatin's lecture, and Sunday Cowboy Church. VIP adds priority/closer parking, a Friday farm-to-table lunch with speakers including Joel Salatin, conference t-shirt, and bonus items.
highlights: Sunday morning Cowboy Church with Joel Salatin
VIP Friday farm-to-table lunch with speakers

social_posts.facebook:
🌾 Modern Homesteading Conference (4th Annual)
📍 Kootenai County Fairgrounds — Coeur d'Alene, ID
🗓️ June 26–27, 2026 (plus Sunday morning Cowboy Church)

Two full days of getting your hands dirty alongside folks who actually live this life. The homestead community gathers in north Idaho this June for live butchering demos both days, food preservation workshops, animal care, gardening sessions, and a packed homestead marketplace.

The lineup is something else. Joel Salatin and Daniel Salatin on regenerative farming. Sally Fallon Morell on nourishing traditions. Mary Heffernan on raising a ranching family. Joe Lamp'l, Dr. Ken Berry, Dr. Patrick Jones, Don Tipping, Anne of All Trades, and more — 40+ practitioners ready to share what they know.

New for 2026: a dedicated Youth Track for ages 12–18, a Friday Community Meet & Greet to find your people, and a Sunday morning Cowboy Church led by Joel Salatin (included with every ticket).

Bring the whole crew — General Admission is $149, kids 3–17 are $49, and the Family Pass covers 2 adults plus up to 10 kids for $399. Food trucks on-site both days.

Early-bird discounts are live right now. Grab your tickets before they climb 👉 https://modernhomesteading.com

#Farm2Table #HomesteadLife #RegenerativeAg #HomesteadingCommunity

events.name: The Homestead Conference (Ploughshare Institute)

dates.notes: Friday–Saturday. Friday 7 AM–7 PM, Saturday 7 AM–6 PM. NOTE: This event has already concluded (today is 2026-06-01). The official site now thanks 2026 attendees and points to the 2027 conference (April 23–24, 2027).

events.description: The Homestead Conference, a production of The Ploughshare Institute for Sustainable Culture, is a gathering of artisans and agrarians bringing together accomplished homesteaders and award-winning craftsmen for hands-on workshops, seminars, and demonstrations on homesteading and self-sufficiency skills. The 2026 edition included a Friday-evening concert with Grammy Award-winning artist Rory Feek, plus seminars and demos on topics such as chicken/lamb processing, hand-milking, gardening for Southern homesteaders, sourdough breadmaking, sheep care, seed saving, and traditional crafts.
events.ticket_price: Not publicly listed as a fixed figure; tickets sold via Etix (etix.com/ticket/v/35495/the-homestead-conference). One-day tickets were available at the entrance. Early-bird pricing was offered after the 2025 event.
highlights: Joel & Daniel Salatin sessions and book signing
Abundant Life interview with Rory Feek

social_posts.facebook:
That's a wrap on The Homestead Conference 2026 at the Heritage Center in Waco, TX — and what a couple of days it was.

April 24–25, folks in the homestead movement gathered for hands-on learning that actually gets your hands dirty: chicken and lamb processing, hand-milking, sourdough breadmaking, seed saving, sheep care, and gardening tuned for Southern homesteaders. Joel and Daniel Salatin brought their pasture wisdom (and a book signing), Shawn and Beth Dougherty talked self-sufficient living, and Bevin Cohen, Butch Tindell, Noah Sanders, Grady Phelan, Dr. Ben Edwards, and Randy Oakley rounded out a lineup of real practitioners sharing real skills.

And Friday night? Grammy winner Rory Feek played a concert that had the whole community singing along under the Texas sky, followed by his Abundant Life interview.

If you were there, you know the magic — the marketplace, the conversations between sessions, the feeling of finding your people.

If you missed it, here's the good news: the 2027 conference is already on the calendar for April 23–24. Mark it down and plan to join the gathering.

Learn more and watch for 2027 details at thehomesteadconference.com

#Farm2Table #HomesteadLife #RegenerativeAg #HomesteadingCommunity

events.name: The Homestead Festival

dates.notes: Friday June 5 & Saturday June 6, 2026. This is the 5th Annual Homestead Festival. Gates open at 7:00 am CT; first speaker at 9:00 am CT, last speaker ends ~4:30 pm CT; evening music ~5:00 pm–9:00 pm CT. (One local news source listed hours as 9am–8pm both days.) RV check-in begins Thursday June 4 after 5 PM CT.

location.venue: The Homestead at Hardison Mill (Rory Feek's 100-acre farm); festival entrance at 4765 Hardison Mill Rd

events.description: The 5th Annual Homestead Festival is a two-day event on Rory Feek's 100-acre historic farm (The Homestead at Hardison Mill) in Columbia, TN, about 45 minutes south of Nashville. It combines hands-on homesteading education from 25+ speakers/teachers with live music, 200+ vendors, demonstrations (wood turning, leather crafting, animal care), food trucks, and a family-friendly 'Lil' Homesteaders Area' (face painting, corn pit, bounce house). The festival runs rain or shine.
events.ticket_price: Two-day passes only (non-refundable, non-transferable). General Admission starts at $95; children ages 6–17 are $25 (must be accompanied by an adult); kids 5 and under free. GA+ and VIP upgrade tiers available. GA+ adds shaded tent seating, free water/sunscreen, upgraded restrooms, and speaker meet-and-greets. VIP adds Homestead Hall access, reserved seating at all tents and Main Stage, VIP entrance parking, festival goodie bag, and breakfast both days.
highlights: live music each evening and during lunch
demos: wood turning, leather crafting, animal care
Lil' Homesteaders Area (face painting, corn pit, bounce house)
limited RV parking (50 spots, no hookups)

social_posts.facebook:
🌾 The 5th Annual Homestead Festival
📅 Friday, June 5 & Saturday, June 6, 2026
📍 The Homestead at Hardison Mill — Rory Feek's 100-acre farm, 4765 Hardison Mill Rd, Columbia, TN (about 45 minutes south of Nashville)
Gates open 7:00 am CT, first speaker at 9:00 am, evening music runs till 9:00 pm. Rain or shine.

There's something about walking onto a working farm with 200+ vendors, woodsmoke from the food trucks, and the sound of a fiddle warming up that reminds folks in the movement why they started homesteading in the first place.

This year the lineup is something special. Joel and Daniel Salatin on pasture-raised everything. Temple Grandin on animal care. John Chester of The Biggest Little Farm. Shawn & Beth Dougherty on self-sufficiency. Darryl Patton on foraging. Plus 25+ teachers running hands-on demos — wood turning, leather crafting, butchering, cheesemaking. The kids get their own corner too: face painting, a corn pit, and a bounce house in the Lil' Homesteaders Area.

Then the sun goes down and Lee Greenwood, The Isaacs, Terri Clark, and Shenandoah take the stage.

Two-day passes start at $95. Kids 6–17 are $25, and 5 & under get in free.

Grab your passes and the full schedule at 👉 https://thehomesteadfestival.com

#HomesteadLife #RegenerativeAg #HomesteadingCommunity #GrowYourOwn

x.lead: 🌾 The 5th Annual Homestead Festival — June 5–6, 2026 on Rory Feek's 100-acre farm in Columbia, TN. Two days of hands-on skills, 200+ vendors, and live music under the stars. Tickets from $95 👉 https://thehomesteadfestival.com

events.name: West Coast Homesteading Expo

dates.notes: Two-day event, Saturday March 14 - Sunday March 15, 2026. Attendee doors open Saturday at 10 AM; show runs through Sunday 3 PM. Organizer has announced two West Coast Homesteading Expos for 2027.

location.venue: Washington State Fair Event Center (Agriplex and Tractor Arena)

events.description: The all-new West Coast Homesteading Expo, produced by Bird + Duck Media, features workshops with expert speakers, live demonstrations, homestead-related product sales, livestock/backyard animals, giveaways, vendors, and the famous Fair Scone truck. Topics span micro farming, urban gardening, gardening, landscaping, greenhouses, baking, sourdough making, seeds, herbalism, foraging, and livestock. Aimed at beginners and current homesteaders, whether living in a city or on acreage; family-oriented.
speakers: Amie Torkelson (founder of an urban farm in Spokane, WA; urban farming/raising animals in the city)
Kristyn Walch (herbalist, Sacred Oak Apothecary; essential homestead herbs)
Kody Hanner (founder of The Homestead Education; teaching children self-sufficiency)
Karl Scheuerman (General Manager of Palouse Heritage; heritage/landrace/ancient grains)
Ashley (chef, forager, nature/forest therapy guide; foraging spring wild foods, nettles cooking demo)
events.ticket_price: Early Bird (until 02/14/26): $10 single / $25 family. Advanced presale (until 03/13/26): $15 single / $30 family. At-door general admission: $20 single / $35 family. Family tickets admit 4 people; children 12 and under free.
events.changes_noted: Event VERIFIED. Key correction: the official event website is https://www.westcoasthomesteadingexpo.com/ (produced by Bird + Duck Media); the previously recorded URL (homesteadliving.com) is a media company that merely lists the event, not the organizer. Confirmed dates of March 14-15, 2026. Corrected venue name from 'Puyallup Fairgrounds' to the official 'Washington State Fair Event Center' (Agriplex and Tractor Arena) at 110 9th Ave SW, Puyallup, WA 98371. Added ticket pricing tiers, registration URL, five named speakers, expanded description, and additional highlights. Added confirmed Facebook page. Set verified: true.

events.name: Country Living & Modern Homesteading Expo

dates.notes: Single-day event, Saturday January 31, 2026, 9:00 AM–4:15 PM. Online registration closes January 21, 2026.

events.description: The 2026 Country Living & Modern Homesteading Expo is the Northwest's premier hands-on learning event for farmers, gardeners, homesteaders, and rural dreamers, offering 100+ hands-on workshops, expert-led classes, demonstrations, and community panels covering every aspect of rural life. Presented by the Livestock Master Foundation with WSU Skagit & Snohomish County Extension and the Cattlemen's Association, hosted by Stanwood FFA.

social_posts.facebook:
📍 Country Living & Modern Homesteading Expo
🗓️ Saturday, January 31, 2026 | 9:00 AM–4:15 PM
🏫 Stanwood High School, Stanwood, WA
🎟️ $85 advance (online) / $100 day-of — online registration closes January 21

If you've been dreaming about a more hands-on, self-sufficient life, this is the gathering to make it real. The folks behind the Northwest's longest-running homesteading event (you might remember it as the Country Living Expo & Cattlemen's Winterschool) are back with 100+ hands-on workshops covering just about every corner of rural life.

We're talking gardening and food preservation, fermentation, raising poultry, keeping bees, regenerative design, off-grid skills, and working with livestock. Whether you're starting your first raised bed or butchering your own hogs, there's a class with your name on it.

Presented by the Livestock Master Foundation with WSU Skagit & Snohomish County Extension and the Cattlemen's Association, hosted by the Stanwood FFA — this is real, practical know-how passed person to person, plus a vendor marketplace to browse and a whole community of homesteaders to meet.

This is where the movement gathers to learn, share, and put down roots together.

Lock in advance pricing before January 21 👉 https://extension.wsu.edu/skagit/country-living-expo/

#Farm2Table #HomesteadLife #RegenerativeAg #SelfSufficiency #HomesteadingCommunity

events.name: Homesteaders of Iowa Expo & Midwest Conference

dates.notes: Vendor load-in/setup on Thursday, February 26th. Doors open 7:45 AM, sessions begin 8:15-8:20 AM both days; classes end 5:30 PM, workshops end 4:30 PM.

events.description: The 2026 Homesteaders of Iowa Expo & Midwest Conference is a family-friendly two-day event bringing together experts, educators, and families for hands-on learning and community building. It features 30 educational classes covering gardening, livestock, food preservation, preparedness, herbalism, and sustainable living, plus 24 hands-on workshops. All activities are held indoors at the Bridge View Center, with a vendor hall, live demonstrations, and national and local speakers.
speakers: David Ford (regenerative living/homesteading; Jefferson County, Iowa)
Jeremy Skinner (Event Chaplain; leads 'Faith and the Homestead' panel)
Pearlicia Cloke (Raw Americana; 'Drama-Free Soap Making' workshop)
David Mixdorf (Prairie Hilltop Farm; 'Raising Chickens for Eggs & Meat')
Dr. Burgmeier (veterinarian, certified in veterinary chiropractic care)
events.ticket_price: General admission required for all speaker sessions, with optional add-on workshops; early bird discount of 10% applied automatically through December 25th. Vendor-hall-only 'shop' day pass is $5 per person (ages 15+) at the door. Vendor booths $200 (10'x10', 1 table + 2 chairs), early bird $125 with code through December 31st; electricity available for $35.

social_posts.facebook:
🌾 Homesteaders of Iowa Expo & Midwest Conference
📅 February 27–28, 2026
📍 Bridge View Center, Ottumwa, IA

Two full days, all indoors, all hands-on. We're talking 30 educational classes and 24 workshops covering gardening, livestock, food preservation, preparedness, herbalism, and sustainable living.

Here's where it gets good. David Ford is bringing real regenerative living wisdom from right here in Jefferson County. David Mixdorf of Prairie Hilltop Farm is walking folks through raising chickens for eggs AND meat. Pearlicia Cloke of Raw Americana is leading a "Drama-Free Soap Making" workshop (yes, that's the actual name, and yes, we're here for it). Lynford Miller from Gingerich Greenhouse has seed starting covered, and Beckham is getting into fruit trees, grafting, and bonsai. There's even veterinary chiropractic care with Dr. Burgmeier.

This is the kind of weekend the homesteading community shows up for — kids welcome, vendor hall full of good folks, and the chance to learn a real skill from people who actually do this work.

Early bird saves you 10% automatically through December 25th. Just want to browse the vendor hall? There's a $5 shop-only day pass at the door.

Grab your spot 👉 https://homesteadersofiowa.com

#Farm2Table #HomesteadingCommunity #RegenerativeAg #BackToBasics

events.name: Ozarks Homesteading Expo

dates.notes: Friday-Saturday. Confirmed via official SimpleTix listing and the organizer's Facebook page. Note: in prior years (2024, 2025) the event ran Sept 6-7 and Sept 5-6 respectively, so 2026 has shifted to Sept 4-5.

location.venue: Ellis O. Jackson Park / Webster County Fairgrounds, 614 North Marshall Street

events.description: An in-person, two-day educational resource fair featuring 40+ teaching sessions, demonstrations, workshops, food trucks, and a large homesteading vendor marketplace. Running since 2019. Topics include livestock raising and processing, canning, beekeeping, cheesemaking, bread making, soap making, foraging, herbal medicine, soil health, solar/off-grid living, fermenting, all-season gardening, orchards, and blacksmithing.
events.ticket_price: $69 General Admission with video access to all 2026 videos (discounted ~50% off when purchased in advance); kids 11 and under free but require a ticket
highlights: large vendor marketplace (150+ vendors in recent years)

social_posts.facebook:
🌿 Ozarks Homesteading Expo | September 4–5, 2026
Ellis O. Jackson Park / Webster County Fairgrounds — 614 North Marshall Street, Marshfield, MO

Mark the calendar, folks. The 2026 dates have shifted a touch earlier this year — Friday and Saturday, September 4th and 5th — so don't let the old September dates trip you up.

For seven years running, this two-day resource fair has been one of the cornerstones of the homesteading movement in the Midwest. Last year drew around 3,000 people to the fairgrounds, and it's easy to see why. We're talking 40+ teaching sessions and hands-on demos covering the real skills: natural beekeeping, foraging, livestock raising and processing, sourdough and fermenting, mushrooms, blacksmithing, and off-grid solar living. There's a vendor marketplace that's grown to 150+ booths, food trucks to keep you fed, and plenty of kids' activities so the whole family can come learn together.

Whether you're just starting to grow your own or you've been canning and cheesemaking for decades, this is where folks in the movement come to swap knowledge and find their people.

🎟️ General Admission is $69 and includes video access to all 2026 sessions — and buying in advance saves you roughly 50%. Kids 11 and under are free but still need a ticket.

Grab yours before the early pricing ends 👉 https://ozarkshomesteading.com

#Farm2Table #HomesteadingCommunity #RegenerativeAg #GrowYourOwn #SelfSufficiency

events.name: Willow Haven Farm Homesteading Conference

dates.notes: Single-day on-farm event. Date confirmed on official Willow Haven Farm site. Past editions ran roughly 8:00 am–4:00 pm and opened with an optional 8:00 am Traditional Catholic Mass followed by a 9:00 am farm breakfast.

events.description: An on-farm, single-day homesteading conference at Willow Haven Farm in New Tripoli, PA, run by Farmer Reuben and Tessa DeMaster. Attendees choose from hands-on workshops and in-depth farm tours covering sustainable living and traditional skills such as sourdough baking, fermentation/food preservation, foraging, herbal remedies, composting, permaculture, and organic gardening. Registration is limited.
events.ticket_price: TBD for 2026; not yet published. For reference, the 2025 edition priced Early Bird Full Day +2 meals at ~$97.34, Full Day +2 meals at ~$113.89, Half-Day at ~$49.87, and a kids ticket (incl. breakfast) at ~$7.18 (all incl. fees).
highlights: choose-your-path morning and afternoon workshop sessions
in-depth farm tours with Farmer Reuben
topics include sourdough, fermentation, foraging, herbal remedies, permaculture, composting
optional 8:00 am Traditional Catholic Mass and farm breakfast

social_posts.facebook:
Willow Haven Farm Homesteading Conference
📅 Saturday, September 5, 2026 (8am–4pm)
📍 Willow Haven Farm, 7686 Herber Rd, New Tripoli, PA

There's something special about learning a skill with your hands in the dirt, surrounded by folks who get it. That's what Farmer Reuben and Tessa DeMaster have built out in New Tripoli — a single-day, on-farm gathering where you choose your own path through morning and afternoon workshops.

Think sourdough that actually rises, fermentation and food preservation that fills your pantry, foraging walks, herbal remedies, composting, permaculture, and organic gardening. Plus in-depth farm tours with Reuben himself, who can talk you through what regenerative looks like in practice — not just in theory.

The day kicks off with an optional 8am Traditional Catholic Mass and a farm breakfast before the learning begins. It's the kind of event the homesteading community keeps coming back to.

Heads up: seats are limited and registration fills fast. The 2026 workshop lineup and pricing are still being finalized, so now's the time to get on it and watch for details.

Reserve your spot 👉 https://willowhavenfarmpa.com/events

#Farm2Table #HomesteadLife #RegenerativeAg #GrowYourOwn #HomesteadingCommunity

events.name: Alaska Homestead Expo & Marketplace (3rd Annual)

dates.notes: Two in-person days (Fri & Sat of Memorial Day weekend). Condensed from a longer format this year in response to attendee feedback; some sources list May 23-25 or May 22-25, but organizer and recent reporting confirm the two in-person days are Friday May 22 and Saturday May 23, with additional virtual Zoom sessions extending into the summer. A Christian church service is scheduled for Sunday at the Common Ground Alaska property, open to ticketholders. Note: at least one local outlet (Mat-Su Sentinel) referred to this as the 'final' Alaska Homestead Expo — worth monitoring.

location.venue: Big Lake Lions Club (Recreation Center), 2942 S Lions Ct

events.description: The 3rd Annual Alaska Homestead Expo & Marketplace brings together Alaskans who want to garden, raise animals, learn traditional skills, and live more self-reliantly. Held over Memorial Day weekend at the Big Lake Lions Club, the event features dozens of Alaskan instructors, hands-on workshops and demonstrations (including whole-cow butchery and iron forging), onsite food trucks, a family learning program, and a curated marketplace of 100-150+ local Alaska vendors. Keynote by Josh & Carolyn Thomas of Homesteading Family. Hosted by Common Ground Alaska, a teaching farmstead in Big Lake (~45 minutes from Anchorage).
events.ticket_price: Full Weekend (in-person/hybrid incl. Zoom sessions): $85; Single Day: $30; Family Pass (2 adults + up to 6 children): $300; Virtual-only (livestream + Zoom): $45; Marketplace-only: $5
highlights: Keynote by Josh & Carolyn Thomas (Homesteading Family)
30+ class offerings across 5-6 learning spaces per day
Food fermentation, medicinal herbs, livestock care, off-grid systems
Family learning tent and teen program
Lady Homesteaders of AK VIP Lunch

events.name: Prairie Roots Homesteading Summit

dates.notes: Two-day event; presentations run 8:30 a.m.–5 p.m. Dates confirmed by official site, ND Tourism, and multiple news outlets.

events.description: The Prairie Roots Homesteading Summit is a two-day regional conference dedicated to self-sufficiency, sustainable living, and traditional skills, bringing together homesteaders, farmers, gardeners, makers, and learners from across the Upper Midwest. Sponsored by the nonprofit McKenzie County Ag Expo Foundation, it features keynote speakers, hands-on demonstrations, informational talks, and exhibitor booths covering soil health, regenerative practices, livestock care, gardening, food production and preservation, herbalism, conservation, community building, and healthy living.
events.ticket_price: $65 general admission; $10 early-bird discount for registrations before May 1, 2026

social_posts.facebook:
🌾 Prairie Roots Homesteading Summit
📍 McKenzie County Ag Expo, Watford City, ND
📅 May 15–16, 2026 | 8:30 a.m.–5 p.m.
🎟️ $65 general admission ($10 early-bird discount before May 1)

Two full days of hands-on homesteading across the Upper Midwest prairie. This is the kind of gathering where you walk in curious and walk out with dirt under your nails and a notebook full of skills you'll actually use.

Justin & Rebekah Rhodes are headlining, along with Tim & Sophia Eng — practitioners who live this life, not just talk about it. Expect real demonstrations on soil health and regenerative practices, food preservation, herbalism, livestock care, and gardening, plus a floor full of exhibitors and makers from across the region.

The best part? You'll be standing shoulder to shoulder with folks in the homesteading community who are figuring out the same things you are — how to grow more, preserve more, and lean a little less on the grocery store every season. Bring the family. Bring your questions. Bring an empty trunk for whatever you find at the exhibitor booths.

Grab your tickets and lock in that early-bird discount before May 1 👉 https://tickets.mcagexpo.com/p/tickets

#Farm2Table #HomesteadLife #RegenerativeAg #HomesteadingCommunity #SelfSufficiency

events.name: Central Arkansas Homestead Conference

dates.notes: 2-day event. Confirmed April 17-18, 2026 via official Kerr Center event listing and Homestead Living 2026 events roundup. A 2nd Annual conference is listed for April 16, 2027 at the same venue.

events.description: Two-day homestead expo at the Conway Expo & Event Center featuring expert speakers, hands-on workshops, and live demonstrations covering gardening, livestock, food preservation, and self-sufficiency. Keynote speaker is Joel Salatin of Polyface Farms. Includes vendors, food trucks, and networking opportunities.
speakers: Taunya Ernst (University of Arkansas Extension, High Tunnel & Urban Ag)
highlights: Hands-on workshops and live demonstrations (incl. chainsaw safety, first aid)
Speakers on beekeeping, rotational grazing, high tunnels, cheesemaking, meat goats
Vendors selling homemade goods, honey, plants, sourdough, homestead supplies
Arkansas Shepherds Association and other associations on-site

social_posts.facebook:
📍 Central Arkansas Homestead Conference
🗓️ April 17–18, 2026
🏠 Conway Expo & Event Center, Conway, AR

Two full days of hands-on homesteading, packed with the kind of skills you can take straight home to your land.

The keynote? None other than Joel Salatin of Polyface Farms — a name that needs no introduction to folks in the regenerative ag movement. Alongside him is a deep roster of real practitioners: Clint & Joy on cheesemaking and elderberry, Sean Pessarra (the Mindful Farmer) on high tunnels, Pat on raising meat goats, Taunya Ernst from U of A Extension on urban ag, and Tim & Amy on herbal remedies. Hosted by Robbie Ryburn of The Wild Huckleberry.

This is where the homestead community comes together. Live demos on everything from chainsaw safety to first aid. Workshops on beekeeping and rotational grazing. Vendors with honey, plants, sourdough, and homemade goods. Food trucks to keep you fed between sessions. And the Arkansas Shepherds Association on-site to talk shop.

Whether you're just getting your hands in the dirt or you've been at it for years, this is your weekend to learn a skill, find your people, and hear from the best.

Grab your tickets and plan your weekend 👇
https://www.centralarhomesteading.com/tickets

#Farm2Table #HomesteadLife #RegenerativeAg #HomesteadingCommunity #GrowYourOwn

events.description: Second annual Eastern Washington Homesteading Expo, a weekend of hands-on learning, skill demonstrations, expert speakers, and vendors covering gardening, pressure canning, solar power, soap making, medicinal herbs, animal processing (chicken/rabbit/pig), dairy basics, fermenting, freeze drying, and cottage licensing. Founded in 2025 by Susan Cooksey of Palmer Lake Homestead. Family-friendly with kids events and an animal petting area.
events.ticket_price: Not published in available sources; early-bird tickets sold via Tixtree (https://www.tixtree.com/o/eastern-washington-homesteading-expo-3d0f8c42e75e)
highlights: expert speakers and live skill demonstrations

social_posts.facebook:
🌱 Eastern Washington Homesteading Expo — April 24–26, 2026
Okanogan County Fairgrounds (175 Rodeo Trail), Okanogan, WA

The homestead community is gathering again for the second annual EWHE, and this one's shaping up to be a full weekend of hands-on learning. Friday kicks off with a homeschool/kids day, public doors open at 11 a.m., and the night wraps up with a family dance from 8–10 p.m. Saturday runs 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. with booths, demos, and presentations top to bottom.

Folks in the movement will get their hands dirty with real skills: pressure canning, soap making, medicinal herbs, fermenting, freeze drying, dairy basics, solar power, cottage licensing, and animal processing for chicken, rabbit, and pig. Larry Littrell will be on hand talking intentional, small-space homesteading — proof you don't need 40 acres to start growing your own.

Started in 2025 by Susan Cooksey of Palmer Lake Homestead, this expo is built family-first. Bring the kids for the petting area, the dance, and a weekend of meeting your people.

Early-bird tickets are moving — grab yours here: https://www.tixtree.com/o/eastern-washington-homesteading-expo-3d0f8c42e75e

#Farm2Table #HomesteadingCommunity #SelfSufficiency #GrowYourOwn #HomesteadExpo
